title: Realign split Agent Misrepresentation scores to Information Accuracy rubric
description: Realign the split Agent Misrepresentation score set to the Information Accuracy rubric rows from the CSV, update the composite dependencies and explanation order, and verify predictions on the revised phrase-level scores.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Starting CSV-aligned taxonomy realignment. Planned sequence:
1. Rename or repurpose the existing split scores in place.
2. Create the missing High-Pressure Tactics phrase score.
3. Update the composite dependency list and explanation order.
4. Run targeted direct predictions for the revised phrase scores and the composite.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented the Information Accuracy realignment in Plexus. Renamed/repurposed the six existing split scores, created Agent Misrepresentation: High-Pressure Tactics, and rewired Agent Misrepresentation (Composite) to the seven CSV-aligned phrase names in rubric order. Direct no-cache predictions now execute successfully for all seven phrase scores using explicit version IDs. Validation highlights: item 308889128 returns a single Savings Promises failure; item 308639116 returns Copay Guarantees, False Carrier Claims, and False Service Claims failures under the new stricter rubric. Composite no-cache execution still fails because the dependency resolver requires championVersionId on these newly created scores; that known platform issue remains tracked separately in plx-b2349b.
